original_image_tags: A red ink stamp with the text "RECORDED" above "EX-37" and "FBI" centered above the date "36 SEP 24 1947".
A large red ink file number stamped "162-83894-112" with "FBI" centered below it. A handwritten "h" in red ink follows the number.
A diagonal rubber stamp in blue ink reads "EX-37 ENCLOSEMENT BEHIND FILE".
A red ink stamp in the bottom left corner reads "53 OCT 3 1947". To its left, "12 TONE" is handwritten in pencil.
A red ink stamp in the bottom left reads "COPIES DESTROYED / 270 NOV 18 1964".
A red ink stamp in the bottom right corner reads "FILEY".

elements.description: Page consists entirely of typed text and various official stamps and handwritten annotations. Two rectangular white holes are punched at the top. Stamps include 'COPIES DESTROYED 270 NOV 18 1964', 'EX-37 - ENCLOSURE BEHIND FILE', 'RECORDED EX-37', 'FBI 36 SEP 24 1947', '53 OCT 3 1947', 'P 344', 'FIVE B', and '162-83894-112'. A handwritten note 'Ref to Major Cart...' is visible in blue ink. A diagonal red stamp '162-83894-112' is also present.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the presence and quoted text of the main stamps (COPIES DESTROYED, EX-37, RECORDED EX-37, FBI 36 SEP 24 1947, 53 OCT 3 1947, 162-83894-112), but Description A lists more stamps (e.g., '12 TONE', 'FILEY') and omits others mentioned by B ('P 344', 'FIVE B') without direct contradiction on the quoted text of shared elements.
